variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_pal && printf 'A man a plan a canal Panama
racecar
hello

Was it a car or a cat I saw?
No lemon, no melon
' > /tmp/eval_pal/input.txt && printf 'true
true
false
true
true
true
' > /tmp/eval_pal/expected.txt
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.py â€” a Python program that reads lines from stdin and for each line writes 'true' (lowercase) on its own line if the line is a palindrome considering only alphanumeric characters (case-insensitive), or 'false' otherwise. The empty line is a palindrome (true).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pal && python3 pal.py < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.go (package main) that reads lines from stdin and for each line writes 'true' (lowercase) on its own line if the line is a palindrome considering only alphanumeric characters (case-insensitive), or 'false' otherwise. The empty line is a palindrome (true). Compile with `go build -o pal pal.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pal && go build -o pal pal.go && ./pal < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.c â€” a C program that reads lines from stdin (use fgets or getline; lines may have any length up to 4096) and for each one writes 'true' or 'false' (lowercase) on its own line. A line is a palindrome if its alphanumeric characters (case-insensitive) read the same forward and backward. The empty line is a palindrome. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o pal pal.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pal && gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o pal pal.c && ./pal < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.rs â€” same I/O contract as the other variants (see Python/Go/C/C++ siblings). Idiomatic Rust 2021+. Will be compiled with `rustc -O pal.rs -o pal` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_pal && printf 'A man a plan a canal Panama
racecar
hello

Was it a car or a cat I saw?
No lemon, no melon
' > /tmp/eval_pal/input.txt && printf 'true
true
false
true
true
true
' > /tmp/eval_pal/expected.txt
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.zig â€” same I/O contract as the other variants. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return â€” output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast pal.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pal && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast pal.zig && ./pal < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
